description: Create a content slide on multi-speed economies with a full-width grouped vertical bar chart showing business investment, consumer spending, and exports across countries by region, plus callout annotations.

header.headline: Many countries are seeing a multi-speed growth dynamic, with tariffs weighing on consumer and export-facing areas of the economy, while AI-linked capex and industries excel
header.sub: Key components of GDP growth dynamics in H1 2025

main.title: Percent growth in H1 2025 (annualized)
main.body:
Grouped bar chart comparing business investment, consumer spending, and exports across countries in the Americas, Europe, and APAC.

Americas — US: business investment 8.4, consumer spending 1.5, exports -0.8; Canada: business investment -3.8, consumer spending 2.5, exports -12.0; Mexico: business investment -6.5, consumer spending 2.1, exports 4.3.

Europe — UK: business investment 6.1, consumer spending 0.7, exports 3.3; Germany: business investment -1.0, consumer spending 1.4, exports 4.7; France: business investment -0.7, consumer spending -0.4, exports -2.1.

APAC — India: business investment 10.0, consumer spending 7.6, exports -0.8; Japan: business investment 1.2, consumer spending 3.7, exports 7.3; Australia: business investment 2.8, consumer spending 0.8, exports 3.3; South Korea: business investment -2.9, consumer spending 0.7, exports 8.0.

Callouts: The US is leading the global AI capex boom and getting a huge GDP lift from it, counterbalancing slowing consumer spend. Tariffs and competitive pressures from China are stifling Germany’s manufacturing investment and exports, making the economy increasingly reliant on tepid consumer spend.
